name: Localazy
what_it_does: Modern localization management platform with a REST API and CLI for automating app translation workflows with AI-assisted translation across 80+ languages.
use_cases: Automatically upload source strings from CI/CD pipelines and download translated files for each release
Trigger AI-assisted machine translation for new strings and route them to human reviewers via API
Query translation status and completeness for each language before gating a production deployment
Manage translation memory and glossary terms programmatically to ensure brand consistency
not_for: Document translation workflows such as marketing PDFs or Word files; Localazy is focused on software strings
Teams with fewer than 1,000 source strings who find a free tool like Weblate sufficient
best_when: Best when development teams want to automate the full localization lifecycle from string extraction to translated build artifact within existing CI/CD pipelines.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need translation of unstructured long-form content rather than app UI strings.

auth.notes: Read and write tokens are generated per project from the Localazy dashboard. OAuth2 is available for user-facing integrations. Project-scoped tokens limit blast radius of key exposure.

pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $19/month for more projects and strings; team and enterprise tiers available
pricing.notes: Free tier is useful for small apps or evaluation. Paid plans scale by source string count and team size.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply per token; documented in API reference per endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with descriptive messages. CLI provides human-readable output with clear error causes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: File uploads are idempotent when using the same file key; translations are overwritten on re-upload. Download operations are fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Project ID and file ID are required for most API calls and must be retrieved via separate list endpoints before use
AI machine translation is asynchronous; agents must poll for completion before downloading results
CLI config file (localazy.json) must exist in the project for CLI commands to function; agents must generate this file before using the CLI

security_score_components.security_notes: Project-scoped tokens limit blast radius. OAuth2 available for user-delegated access. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. TLS enforced.
